payload.description: Deploy full stack containerized project to Azure Cloud using Terraform. Uses (ACR + Linux WebApp)

issue.title: fix: conditionally emit CORS headers only when CORS_ORIGIN is set
issue.body:
The nginx template was unconditionally emitting `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` with a literal `${CORS_ORIGIN}` value — even when unset — and the README documented behavior that didn't match.

## Changes

- **`src/web/Dockerfile`**: Replace unconditional `add_header` directives with a build-time shell conditional that only writes CORS headers into the nginx config when `CORS_ORIGIN` is non-empty:
  ```dockerfile
  $([ -n "${CORS_ORIGIN}" ] && printf \
    "        add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin'  '${CORS_ORIGIN}' always;\n \
            add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' 'GET, POST, OPTIONS' always;\n \
            add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' '...' always;")
  ```

- **`README.md`**: Update the CORS behaviour table to accurately reflect that an empty `CORS_ORIGIN` emits no headers (`_(none)_`), and that a set value emits all three CORS headers (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin` + Methods + Headers).

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

🔒 GitHub Advanced Security automatically protects Copilot coding agent pull requests. You can protect all pull requests by enabling Advanced Security for your repositories. [Learn more about Advanced Security.](https://gh.io/cca-advanced-security)

comment.body:
> The nginx template was unconditionally emitting `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` with a literal `${CORS_ORIGIN}` value — even when unset — and the README documented behavior that didn't match.
> 
> ## Changes
> * **`src/web/Dockerfile`**: Replace unconditional `add_header` directives with a build-time shell conditional that only writes CORS headers into the nginx config when `CORS_ORIGIN` is non-empty:
>   ```dockerfile
>   $([ -n "${CORS_ORIGIN}" ] && printf \
>     "        add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin'  '${CORS_ORIGIN}' always;\n \
>             add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' 'GET, POST, OPTIONS' always;\n \
>             add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' '...' always;")
>   ```
> * **`README.md`**: Update the CORS behaviour table to accurately reflect that an empty `CORS_ORIGIN` emits no headers (`_(none)_`), and that a set value emits all three CORS headers (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin` + Methods + Headers).
> 
> 🔒 GitHub Advanced Security automatically protects Copilot coding agent pull requests. You can protect all pull requests by enabling Advanced Security for your repositories. [Learn more about Advanced Security.](https://gh.io/cca-advanced-security)

comment.diff_hunk: @@ -50,3 +51,38 @@ No resources. | [location](#output\_location) | The location of the resource. | | [web](#output\_web) | Web frontend hostname and deployment details. | + +## CORS Configuration + +The web frontend proxies API requests through nginx (`/api` → `API_URI`). Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) headers are baked into the nginx config at **Docker build time** via the `CORS_ORIGIN` build argument. + +### Behaviour + +| `CORS_ORIGIN` value | `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` emitted | Effect | +| --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | +| _(empty, default)_ | _(no header)_ | Most restrictive — browser blocks cross-origin requests | +| `https://myapp.azurewebsites.net` | `https://myapp.azurewebsites.net` | Only that exact origin is allowed | +
comment.body: @copilot open a new pull request to apply changes based on [this feedback](https://github.com/atrakic/azure-full-stack-terraform/pull/16#discussion_r2913928095)

Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) headers are baked into the nginx config at **Docker build time** via the `CORS_ORIGIN` build argument. + +### Behaviour + +| `CORS_ORIGIN` value | `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` emitted | Effect | +| --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | +| _(empty, default)_ | _(no header)_ | Most restrictive — browser blocks cross-origin requests | +| `https://myapp.azurewebsites.net` | `https://myapp.azurewebsites.net` | Only that exact origin is allowed | +
